_meta.author: refine-scorecard skill
_meta.note: Evidence-based finalization of Adam Botana (R), FL House HD-80 (Lee/Collier, Bonita Springs). Currency verified 2026-06-20: still sitting HD-80 incumbent, running 2026 (R primary 8/18/2026; primary challenger + Dem Mitchel Schlayer). Four primary-source roll calls confirmed from FL House vote PDFs (SB 300 6-week, SB 254 trans-care, SB 1718 immigration, plus HB 543 constitutional carry co-intro + floor speech). Catholic; small-business owner (family marina/boat-club + carnival/F&B). Business-moderate reputation; environmental/water focus but no documented anti-GOP roll call deviation found.

set.office: FL House of Representatives, District 80 (sitting; 2026 R primary candidate)

profile.religion: Catholic (per official FL House member page)
profile.background: Small-business owner; VP of family Bay Water Boat Club (marina/boat-livery industry, 15+ yrs); prior carnival and food-and-beverage business. GED. First elected to FL House 2020 (then HD-76, now HD-80); Majority Chief Floor Whip 2022-24; Chair, Natural Resources & Disasters Subcommittee.
profile.confidence_note: Currency web-verified 2026-06-20 vs FL House roster + Ballotpedia. Scored from four confirmed roll-call PDFs (flsenate.gov vote rosters) plus floor speech and NRA endorsement. Several cells null (no public position found) per evidence-only discipline.

adam-botana.notes_append: EVIDENCE DOSSIER 2026-06: HARD roll calls (FL House vote PDFs): Y on SB 300/2023 (6-week heartbeat abortion ban); Y on CS/SB 254/2023 (felony ban on gender-transition care for minors); Y on CS/CS/SB 1718/2023 (immigration enforcement, mandatory E-Verify, sanctuary preemption); co-introducer of HB 543/2023 (constitutional/permitless carry) and spoke on floor calling 2A 'a constitutional God-given right endowed by our creator' (passed 76-32, R majority, he voted with majority). NRA-endorsed incumbent (2022). Catholic. Business-aligned (Chamber/NFIB-endorsed); water-quality/Everglades focus but NO documented anti-GOP-base roll call found. HB 1557/2022 (Parental Rights in Education) passed 69-47 R-majority but individual Botana vote not separately confirmed (SOFT).

0.note: No explicit 'personhood/life-begins-at-conception' statement found; his SB 300 vote restricts but does not abolish abortion.

1.note: Voted Y on SB 300 (6-week restriction) but no vote for/advocacy of abortion ABOLITION; bar is abolition not restriction -> documented contrary to the abolition standard.

2.note: No public position found on embryonic stem cell / IVF embryo discard / chimeric research.

3.note: No public position found on euthanasia / assisted suicide.

4.note: R incumbent; no record of Planned Parenthood/NARAL/EMILY's List/abortion-industry PAC money; voted for 6-week ban (SB 300). No abortion-industry support documented.

0.note: No explicit one-man-one-woman marriage affirmation found.

1.note: No position found on same-sex marriage / civil unions.

2.note: Voted Y on CS/SB 254 (2023) banning gender-transition care for minors -> affirms biological sex / rejects transgender ideology in law. HARD.

3.note: No position found on no-fault divorce reform.

4.note: Voted Y on SB 254 (2023), which restricted promotion of gender ideology incl. in public spheres; opposes LGBTQ+ ideology promotion in policy. HARD (vote).

0.note: FL enacted universal school choice (HB 1 2023) under R majority but Botana's individual vote not separately confirmed; no explicit homeschool-freedom statement found.

1.note: SB 254 (2023) included parental-consent/notification framework on minors' gender care; voted Y. Aligns with parental notification/consent on minors' medical care. HARD (vote).

2.note: HB 1557 (Parental Rights in Education, 2022) passed 69-47 R-majority but Botana's individual vote not separately confirmed; SOFT inference only -> null per discipline.

3.note: No position found on porn age-verification / penalties for sexualizing minors.

4.note: No position found on faith-based adoption/foster agencies.

0.note: No specific record found on right to publicly profess Christ; he is Catholic but no policy statement.

1.note: No record found on conscience exemptions.

2.note: No record found on compelled-speech / pronoun mandates.

3.note: No record found on public prayer / religious displays / Sabbath.

4.note: No record found on state funding of non-Christian displays.

0.note: No specific CBDC-opposition statement found (FL passed anti-CBDC SB 7054 2023 under R majority; individual vote not confirmed).

2.note: No explicit balanced-budget-amendment position; FL has balanced-budget requirement by constitution. Sponsored business-tax-cut bill but that is not a balanced-budget-amendment position -> null.

3.note: No position found on usury limits / tithe-friendly tax structure.

4.note: No specific ESG/WEF-pushback or anti-trust record found. (FL passed anti-ESG HB 3 2023 R-majority; Botana vote not confirmed.)

0.note: No position found on hand-counted paper ballots / opposing voting machines.

2.note: No position found on single-day in-person voting.

3.note: No specific position found on mail-in/drop boxes/ballot harvesting (FL SB 7050 2023 R-majority; vote not confirmed).

4.note: No specific Zuckerbucks/private-election-funding-ban position confirmed for Botana.

0.note: No affirmative pro-police advocacy / anti-defund statement found (bar requires affirmative advocacy, not routine governance).

1.note: No specific cashless-bail / rogue-prosecutor record confirmed for Botana.

4.note: No record found on magistrate's duty / Rom. 13:4 theme.

1.note: No record found declining federal mandates.

2.note: Co-introduced/backed HB 543 constitutional carry (state expansion of 2A vs federal gun-control posture) and voted for state 6-week ban (life) post-Dobbs state-authority context -> resisted federal/centralized control on guns & life at state level. SOFT-to-HARD; supported by votes.

4.note: No record found on federal capture of elections/data/land.

0.note: Border-barrier/military-presence is federal; no specific Botana statement. SB 1718 is state enforcement (see q2/q3).

2.note: Voted Y on CS/CS/SB 1718 (2023), which preempts/bars sanctuary policies and bolsters immigration enforcement. HARD (vote).

3.note: SB 1718 (2023) mandated E-Verify for many employers; voted Y. HARD (vote).

4.note: No position found on birthright citizenship or foreign farmland ownership (FL SB 264 2023 foreign-land R-majority; Botana vote not confirmed).

0.note: Co-introducer of HB 543 (2023) constitutional/permitless carry; spoke in favor on floor ('a constitutional God-given right endowed by our creator'); bill passed 76-32 R-majority, voted with majority. HARD (co-sponsor + floor advocacy).

1.note: No specific record found on red-flag laws/mag limits/AWB; FL's 2018 red-flag law predates him. No documented position -> null.

2.note: NFA/GCA repeal is federal; no state-level Botana statement found.

3.note: No specific castle-doctrine/stand-your-ground vote confirmed (FL law predates him).

4.note: NRA-endorsed; strong constitutional-carry advocacy implies opposition to citizen-disarmament initiatives. SOFT-to-HARD (endorsement + pro-2A floor advocacy).
